feedback.text: Hello! As a french citizen, I believe that the Digital Fairness Act should also involve the protection of digital softwares such as video games from being forcefully discontinued by its publishers. While I understand softwares cannot be maintained forever, I believe that most abandoned softwares could offer "end of line plan", for example they could still be maintained by online communities of users. As someone often purchasing softwares, I want to be sure my costumer rights are protected in a manner that would allow me to use those softwares for as long as possible. I would like to point out that there is a massive demand by EU online users for end of life plan. For example, the "stop destroying video games" initiative has reached more than 1,000,000 signatures.
